author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
pm context and pm next currently assemble their working set from structural rules (dependency-aware actionability per ADR pm-9x6x: ready = active leaf with no open blocked_by, ordered by compareCriticalItems). That is correct but shallow: it uses only a fraction of the data the tracker already records, and it does not adapt between a 10-item hobby workspace and a million-item, decades-spanning one.

Proposal: a pluggable relevance-scoring engine (SDK-first) that ranks candidate items for context assembly by combining ALL available signals:
- recency and activity density from the per-item history streams (recently touched, recently discussed, aging-WIP)
- dependency-graph proximity to the agent's focus item, active claims, and current plan steps
- structural priority signals: priority, risk, severity, deadline pressure, sprint/release membership
- semantic similarity between the agent's focus/query and item corpus (reusing the existing hybrid search providers)
- knowledge density: items carrying learnings/decisions relevant to the working set get surfaced so past lessons are re-injected instead of re-learned
- author affinity: what this agent (PM_AUTHOR) touched before

Design constraints:
- Scoring lives in the SDK as a documented, replaceable interface (scoreContextCandidates); the built-in scorer is just the default implementation — projects can ship their own via extensions.
- Deterministic and explainable: --explain-ranking (or a score breakdown in json output) shows per-signal contributions, so agents and humans can audit why an item was surfaced.
- Token-budgeted assembly: given a budget (explicit flag or config default), the engine packs the highest-value items and degrades gracefully (tighter projections before dropping items).
- O(result) at scale: signal inputs must come from the derived read index (pm-801d) and search index, never a full workspace scan; benchmark on the pm-mi2x synthetic workspaces.
- Small-workspace neutrality: on tiny workspaces the ranking must reduce to today's sensible ordering with zero configuration.

This is the core of the 'project management = context management' philosophy: the optimal context at any time, computed from all data, metadata, and history the workspace already has.
Adaptive context relevance engine: multi-signal scoring over metadata, graph, and history drives context/next assembly under an explicit token budget

SDK exposes a documented pluggable context-scoring interface with the built-in multi-signal scorer as default; pm context/pm next accept a token-budget bound and pack results by score with graceful projection degradation; json output can include a per-signal score breakdown; ranking is deterministic for a fixed workspace state; hot path stays index-backed (no full scan) and meets pm-9rxu latency targets on the 100k and 1M benchmark workspaces; on small workspaces default ordering remains stable (golden tests); extension can replace the scorer end-to-end (exemplar test)

message: Long-horizon context-quality planning: multi-signal relevance gap

author: codex-agent
message: Split adaptive context relevance engine into implementation slices

author: codex-agent

patch.value: SDK-first adaptive ranking engine for pm context/next: derive multi-signal relevance from tracker metadata, graph, history, semantic search, and agent state, then assemble bounded-token context with explainable scores.

message: Backfill concise description after splitting context engine into child slices

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Planning evidence 2026-07-07: all-status duplicate checks showed pm-4k6b is the canonical adaptive context engine parent; no focused child existed for signal extraction, scorer contract, or token-budget packing. Added non-duplicate implementation slices: pm-3hps (derived signal feature store), pm-h3no (pluggable SDK scorer contract), and pm-55ra (diversity-aware token-budget packer). Algorithm sketch: build an index-backed candidate set from focus/claim/query/dependency windows; compute normalized signal vectors for recency, activity density, graph proximity, priority/risk/deadline pressure, semantic similarity, knowledge density, author affinity, and continuity deltas; combine them through a deterministic default scorer with extension override; pack by marginal utility per token while preserving required anchors and degrading projection depth before dropping items; expose score breakdowns for explainability and pm-atfm evaluation.

author: codex-agent
message: Adaptive context engine planning slices recorded and verified via pm-doxj linked tests
